title: Small OOF stack with sigmoid-only regime calibration
summary: Calibration docs note that isotonic can change ROC-AUC by introducing ties, while sigmoid preserves ranking.
rationale: Calibration docs note that isotonic can change ROC-AUC by introducing ties, while sigmoid preserves ranking. Given this competition is scored by ROC-AUC, a narrow stack that blends genuinely different models is safer than aggressive isotonic post-processing.
implementation_hint: Build a 2-3 model OOF stack from the current best GBDT, the survival auxiliary, and a categorical-heavy model. Calibrate each base learner with fold-local sigmoid/Platt scaling only, then learn a tiny meta-model or regime-specific linear blend using `Year_Race`-blocked OOF predictions. Keep raw-vs-calibrated ablations because AUC may already be optimal without calibration.
expected_effect: Better public-board robustness from diversified decision boundaries while preserving AUC ranking.
risk: If the base models are too correlated, the stack adds noise; isotonic should be avoided unless it proves neutral on AUC in OOF.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
